_comment: Plan 2 T17 — Junior Officer archetype dialog. Three archetypes: cocky, serious, political. Peer-level tone (officer to officer, not NCO to recruit). Loaded by CompanionDialogueCatalog. Token usage: {PLAYER_NAME} for frequent peer-to-peer address; {PLAYER_RANK} sparingly for officer-tier callouts; {LORD_NAME} for the enlisted lord both peers serve; {COMPANION_NAME}/{COMPANION_FIRST_NAME} in player options addressing the junior officer. Tokens are populated by EnlistedMenuBehavior.SetCompanionConversationTokens before the conversation opens.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: So you're the other officer {LORD_NAME}'s been bragging about. {PLAYER_NAME}, is it? Hope you can keep up — I plan on collecting a banneretcy out of this contract.

options.text: Try not to ride your horse off a cliff first, {COMPANION_NAME}.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: We haven't been introduced, {PLAYER_RANK}. {LORD_NAME} speaks well of you — I look forward to working alongside.

options.text: Likewise, {COMPANION_NAME}. {PLAYER_NAME} — and we'll do good work together.
{PLAYER_NAME}. Let's see how the first muster goes before we get sentimental.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Ah — {PLAYER_NAME}. I've been curious. Let's find a moment to compare notes; {LORD_NAME}'s circle is more interesting than it looks from the outside.

options.text: I'd like that, {COMPANION_NAME}. Plenty I don't know yet.
Let's keep our notes professional, shall we, {COMPANION_NAME}?
options.tooltip: Hold them at arm's length (-1 relationship)

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Good. I like an officer with some teeth, {PLAYER_NAME}. Find me when you've got something worth saying.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Understood, {PLAYER_NAME}. The chain of command is what holds this company together — let's make sure we hold up our part of it.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Excellent, {PLAYER_NAME}. A friendly word at the right ear travels further than a dozen drilled charges. Remember that.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Got something to discuss, {PLAYER_NAME}, or just stretching your legs?

options.text: What's your read on our posture?
What are you hoping to get out of this commission, {COMPANION_NAME}?
Your view on the last engagement?
options.tooltip: Recent battle from a peer's view

options.text: What do you make of the rest of {LORD_NAME}'s officers?
options.tooltip: Discreet gossip on the lord's circle

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Posture? We're posturing too cautiously, {PLAYER_NAME}. Give me half a wing of cavalry and I'd already be flanking. {LORD_NAME}'s playing the long game when the short game would do.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Our line's solid, {PLAYER_NAME}. The flanks need a second cavalry screen if we're committing to open ground — I've been meaning to raise it with {LORD_NAME} at the next council. You should second me.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Tactically? Adequate. Politically? {LORD_NAME}'s choosing engagements that look impressive in a court report — not the ones that win the campaign. Watch where the dispatches go after each fight, {PLAYER_NAME}.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: A banneret's title and a fief I can hunt on, {PLAYER_NAME}. Maybe a song or two with my name in it. I didn't take this commission for the modest pay, that's certain.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: A captaincy of my own, in time. Not for the title — for the responsibility. My father served thirty years and never commanded; I owe him the rank he never reached. You understand that kind of debt, {PLAYER_NAME}.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: A seat at the right table, {PLAYER_NAME}. Titles come and go — proximity to the people who decide where titles fall is what lasts. {LORD_NAME}'s commission is one rung. There are several more.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Did you see me take that flag, {PLAYER_NAME}? Tell me you saw it. Best charge of the season — I'll be hearing about it for weeks, and I intend to enjoy every word.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: We won, {PLAYER_NAME}, but our second line broke later than it should have re-formed. I want to walk the field with you tomorrow and see what we can learn before the next engagement.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: A tidy victory, {PLAYER_NAME}. More importantly, {LORD_NAME}'s rivals were watching. Whatever the dispatches say in a week, the real verdict is being whispered tonight in three different camps.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: The senior captain rides like he's afraid of his own horse. The QM hoards saddle-oil. And honestly, {PLAYER_NAME}, you're more competition than the rest of them combined — keep up, would you?

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: I won't gossip, {PLAYER_NAME}. But I'll say this: two of {LORD_NAME}'s captains are due for promotion within the year, and one of them deserves it. The other should not be commanding anything larger than a baggage cart.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: The senior captain is on his way out — his family's faction lost a key marriage last winter. The chaplain's brother just bought land near {LORD_NAME}'s keep. Read the room accordingly, {PLAYER_NAME}.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Mind your flank, {PLAYER_NAME}. I'll do the same.
